name: Operation Forough-e Javidan (Eternal Light)
description: On 26 July 1988, six days after [Ruhollah Khomeini](/people/ruhollah-khomeini) accepted UN Resolution 598 for a ceasefire in the [Iran–Iraq War](/events/iran-iraq-war), the MEK's [National Liberation Army](/orgs/national-liberation-army-of-iran) launched Operation Forough-e Javidan (Eternal Light) from Iraqi territory with approximately 7,000 fighters. The offensive aimed to capture Kermanshah and march on Tehran. MEK forces initially seized the town of Islamabad-e Gharb but were met with a devastating Iranian counterattack (Operation Mersad). The MEK suffered catastrophic losses — 1,500 to 2,500 killed in three days — and the operation failed completely. This was the last land battle of the Iran–Iraq War and is widely considered to have provided the regime a pretext for the [1988 mass executions](/events/mass-executions-1988) of political prisoners.
editorial_notes: Also known as Operation Mersad (the Iranian counter-operation name). Last battle of Iran-Iraq War.
